title: Parcel Gate Loader
prompt:
Create a production-ready Parcel Gate Loader using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original loader presents three deterministic export gates—Pack, Verify, Send—with a violet parcel tile travelling to the active gate. A native Next gate control advances the state and wraps back to Pack, so the live preview has an intentional interaction instead of an uncontrolled loop. Use a white light-Bento card, #F4F5F6 interior, #ECECEE borders, 22px corners, gentle neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F support copy. Violet is the only bright accent and there are no gradients.

Use role=status with a polite textual progress sentence, numbered gates with current state visible in text, and a native button with a 44px minimum target. The control must work through click, Enter, and Space, with a 3px visible focus outline. Move the parcel tile using a spring at stiffness 320 and damping 25, with a 140ms ease-out status fade and a 90ms press scale. State must be local and deterministic: no timers, random values, browser storage, remote assets, or network requests.

At 320px, retain readable gate labels and a single horizontal track. On larger previews, leave breathing room without fixed placement. Maintain AA contrast and do not rely on violet alone to communicate active progress. For prefers-reduced-motion, snap the parcel to its next gate and use only a short opacity transition. Return complete client code, a runnable CSS-only approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:

1. Pack — Current

*/ .parcel-gate{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.parcel-gate p{padding-top:12px;border-top:2px solid #ececee;color:#111;font-weight:800}.parcel-gate p::before{display:inline-block;width:12px;height:12px;margin-right:8px;border-radius:3px;background:#7557e8;content:""}.parcel-gate button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.parcel-gate *{transition:none}}
